data.current_summary: Get a resolved version from a range or a tag
data.current_description:
Returns the latest version matching `specifier` or `null` if there is no matching version.
Only valid [semver](https://semver.org/) versions are considered.
Stable versions are prioritized over pre-releases.
data.description_suggestion: Resolves a version specifier (range, tag, or exact version) to the latest matching version for the specified GitHub repository. Returns the resolved version string or null if no match exists. Only valid semantic versions are considered. Prioritizes stable releases over pre-releases. Requires a `specifier` query parameter containing the version constraint.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to clarify the `specifier` input parameter requirement and better explain the resolution logic. Added details about what constitutes valid input and the prioritization rules to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description:
Returns a list of all periods for which some stats are available in descending order.
The `links` property of each period indicates which stats are available.
data.summary_suggestion: List available statistics periods and data types
data.description_suggestion: Returns a list of all time periods for which usage statistics are available, ordered in descending chronological order. Each period includes a `links` property that indicates which types of statistics are available for that period (e.g., hits, bandwidth, browser data). Use this endpoint to discover available data before querying specific statistics. Note: Most data has a two-day delay; monthly and yearly summaries have a four-day delay.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic.
